name: Approach
intuition_and_algorithm:
Intuition:
                            Building a binary tree from inorder and postorder traversals follows a recursive strategy
                            similar to the preorder-inorder version. However, instead of taking the root from the start
                            (as in preorder), we take it from the end of the postorder array. This root splits the
                            inorder array into left and right subtrees. Using index boundaries avoids repeated slicing,
                            and a hashmap is used for O(1) root lookups in the inorder traversal.
                        
Approach:

Create a hashmap to store the indices of elements in the inorder array for fast lookup.
                            
Define a recursive function buildTree with parameters for current ranges in
                                inorder and postorder arrays.
Base case: If postStart > postEnd or inStart > inEnd,
                                return null.
The current root is postorder[postEnd]. Find its index in inorder using the
                                map.
Calculate leftSubtreeSize as rootIndex - inStart.
Recursively build left subtree with adjusted inStart to rootIndex-1 and
                                postStart to postStart + leftSubtreeSize - 1.
                            
Recursively build right subtree with inIndex+1 to inEnd and
                                postStart + leftSubtreeSize to postEnd - 1.
                            
Return the root node constructed from the above steps.

complexities.time: O(N), Every node is visited once.
complexities.space: O(N), Due to hashmap and recursion stack (up to tree height).
